column1: Major Numbered Lists in the Lotus Sutra
Details of the Seven Great Parables
-8 The Threefold Lotus Sutra (Kato et al.)

These tables cover the most significant and recurring numbered lists in the Lotus Sutra, including doctrinal categories, parables, assemblies, and enumerations of benefits and beings. If you require a more granular breakdown of any specific list (such as the full enumeration of the twenty-five forms of Avalokiteśvara), please specify.
Four Faiths (Stages of Faith in the Lotus Sutra, Ch. 17)
Six Difficulties (Difficulties in Accepting and Teaching the Lotus Sutra, Ch. 11)
Ten Merits (Merits of Upholding the Lotus Sutra, Ch. 19, 21)
Twenty Benefits (Benefits of Hearing and Reciting the Lotus Sutra, Ch. 19)
Fourteen Malicious Acts (Sins to be Avoided, Ch. 14)
Note: The actual dhāraṇī spells are long incantations; the above are the names and sources as typically listed in commentaries and translations. Some translations may vary in the exact enumeration.
